description_long: This object is a modern traffic light mounted on a tall, cylindrical metallic pole with a wide, flared base for stability. The signal unit is attached perpendicularly to the pole and features a vertical array of five circular signal lights, each with a distinct lens cover, likely for different colored signals. The housing of the signal unit is rectangular and appears to be made of a durable, weather-resistant material, possibly metal or reinforced plastic, with a glossy black finish. The pole itself is metallic gray, with a smooth, slightly reflective surface, and is likely constructed from galvanized steel for strength and corrosion resistance. The control box is visible behind the signal lights, suggesting easy access for maintenance. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, suitable for installation at intersections or pedestrian crossings in urban environments.
description: A tall, metallic pole-mounted traffic light with a vertical array of five signal lights and a visible control box, finished in black and gray. The design is robust and suitable for urban intersections.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full vertical array of five signal lights and the control box attached to the pole.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the signal unit and the protruding control box behind the lights.
description_view_2: Rear view showing mostly the back of the control box and the pole, with the signal lights not visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the profile of the control box and the mounting structure, with the signal lights facing away.
